summary: The Naish Pivot Nvision combines the proven Pivot platform with Aluula and Quadtex materials for a premium all-around kite. Significantly lighter and more responsive than the standard Pivot with extended wind range.
best_for: Premium all-around riding with the latest material technology

sources.summary: Riders note a dramatic improvement over the standard Pivot thanks to Aluula and Quadtex. The weight savings translate directly into better drift and low-end performance.
Side-by-side comparison with the standard Pivot shows the Nvision holding its shape better in gusts and providing noticeably more hangtime.
The Nvision is seen as the ultimate version of the already-popular Pivot. Price is the main discussion point, with most agreeing the performance gains are real.

reviews.full_transcript: Oh, another glorious day here on the Dutch beach in Waz this time. Looks like Stig and the crew have just arrived. Ean is here as well. But you going to move to the Netherlands or what? >> What up, brother? >> Good to see you. >> Sticky. [Music] Amazing to have these legends back on the Vik Beach, one of my favorite spots in the Netherlands as well. Very iconic spot. Of course, you and Jaspine, Nash's Sky Designer, and of course, Tig Hooknav, who does need an introduction. But big action coming up on some new toys that we've got here from Nash. >> Hi, my name is Dez Walsh and I work at Nash and like to show you the new 2026 Monarch. Um, this board has been completely redesigned. This is the second version of this one. What this board is built for is to go fast, be powerful, and get really good pop. So, kind of the perfect board for Ruben Lenton and hopefully a lot of other people out here, too. So, what makes it go fast is got a lower rocker. It's got wide square tips and it's got a fairly um stiff construction. And to get the stiff construction, it has two 10 cm wide UD carbon strips down the board. And the rest of the board is glass. And if you look at it too, it's got a lot of shaping on it on the bottom too to give it extra grip. It has asymmetrical fins so it uh sink your rail into the water even better than the normal fin. And then some of the other features this year with the Apex binding. We're changing this the color here a little bit. And this is the highlight color that you see throughout the whole brand. It's on the bindings. It's on the rail there too. You'll see hits of it on all the kites and everything else. Kind of keep everything looking cohesive. We also have a new handle called the Haptic handle on it. It's got risers there so you can have it either lower or riser ones for board off and just got super easy to grip, easy to control your board for some extreme board offs. Cannot wait to see this board out in the water here. It should be the perfect board for Reuben. We'll see uh how it goes and hopefully he's going to go to the moon. >> The wind is absolutely pumping, so we don't want to wait too long. But before we head out, can you please run me through what you have created? >> Yeah, sounds good. Uh we'll start here with the Q series pivot. So this year the big change is we've gone from for a blended leading edge construction. So moving on from a traditional Dacron that we've used for a few years. We've gone to a really stiff Dacron through the leading edge. As you can see the black dacron is a stiff 160 g dacron which really holds the kite's shape in the air. So in these strong winds we're going to have a super solid art. It's not going to change from 5 knots to 30 knots. >> Nice and direct to where it is. >> But what we did when we found this was super stiff kite. You still need some twist. So, what we've done is you can see in the tips here, the last three segments are a lighter, more flexible Dacron cuz this is where you get the steering from the kite. Makes a nice lighter feel. Twists really well in the steering. And then same with the strut. Same material, lighter weight. So, the canopy can kind of twist while you're steering it. So, you have a kite that sits super rigid, but has a nice light, playful feel, which is really important. >> Amazing. And I mean, the DNA of the Pivot had a king of the air title on its name. Uh, but it's also a more all round kite, right? >> Yeah. So, I want to go out today and kind of do a bit of it all. I want to slash some waves, do some loops, do some free riding. And that's kind of what we really focus in on the pivot is we really want it to master everything. We don't want to kind of push it down two one rabbit hole too far. We introduced the Psycho last year, which is a dedicated big air kite. So, we were, you know, thinking of going, let's make the Pivot that king of the air specialist kite, but then all those people that love the Pivot for what it is are going to be bummed. So, we've doubled down on making it the best all round kite. >> Nice. Well done. >> So, some other cool features this year. Both kites have SK99 bridal lines, which are just the pinnacle of what you want on your bridal lines. Super thin, really strong, low elongation, really direct, cut through the wind well, everything that you want in a good bridal. >> Nice. Not much drag. >> No drag. Super thin. You see this stuff? We've got really nice splicing on it. So, it's really smooth. Nothing to catch on there. Really great bridal lines. >> Yeah. I mean, Nash is known for its quality. So, this looks uh Nash >> Nash quality. >> Amazing. >> Q series. >> Love it. Let's check out the Envision. >> Yeah. >> So, the Envision, I've got to pay more for it. >> Why? >> You got to pay more for it. >> You know why? Cuz it's better. >> Okay. Take the money. >> But, um, basically this year, what we focused on was weight saving. So, we introduced a new closing seam in the kite. Last year, we went for a super bomber closing seam. We saw some brands having some issues with the Lula kites on the first iteration. So, we, you know, we were like, we don't want anything. We spent a lot of time developing these closing seams, which is really important to this material. This year, we were like, okay, we had zero warranties. It was a little overbuilt, maybe, but let's let's try and strip it back. Still holding our really high standards. So, we've got a new closing seam. Overall, the kite's 10% lighter than last year, which is quite a big gain. And then with the new closing seam, you have a kite that kind of twists a little better because it's not as overbuilt in the closing seam. So, you'll notice little lighter bar feel, nicer, kind of smoother turning, and a quicker kite overall because of that twist. Um, we also have the SK9 and bridal lines and kind of more tuned in, more spec precise profiles and bridling on this kite because it's a bit more high performance. So, you know, maybe not better for everyone. As I said, like if you're a a rider that needs a little more forgiveness in your kite, the Q series is probably a little bit more forgiving. This is really meant to be fast and all over the place, spot on, ridden like ridden hard. But one really cool feature that I love most about the Pivot Envision is because of the small light leading edge, you have a kite that drives fast hold in the window. So, lots of D power, quick recovery from kite loops. But then when the kite is unloaded, due to the lightweight small leading edge, the center of weight is further back than before. So you get this kite that not only shoots fast forward, but when it's unloaded, like on a wave, you'll get really cool drift, which is in a high performance kite, really hard to find. >> Wicked. So when you go down the line, the kite just drifts with you. >> It just drifts with you. It's lightweight. The weight's a bit further back, but then when it's loaded up, it wants to drive forward again. >> Let's just hope it doesn't stop on the mid loop. >> Yeah. Won't do that. Nice. I'm looking forward to test it out, man. Ready to to ride hard. And I love this color combo, man. >> Yeah, it looks fresh, huh? >> Wow. >> Nice. Here we go. >> Thank you, man. >> Are you going to come out? >> Of course, I'm going to come out. >> Look at it. Let's hit it. [Applause] That's our new hard shell. It's pretty sick. It's like uh fully formed. Comes around a little more so you get like a really nice spread load instead of um some hard shells on the market. Like they cut the back a little short and then the sides squeeze. >> So this one is really comfortable without getting too big and bulky so you can still move but it has a really nice all way around support. Nice. And it looks dope. I like the design. Black and gold. So, a bit of a game plan. I'm first going to take out the Pivot Q series, the normal Darkron one, and then I will swap around with stickers on for the End Vision. Get some extra performance in there. So, let's do this. Okay. Okay. So, I'm riding the 50 cm bar and also on the slow turning speed. Let's see. Woohoo! Okay. He's going to try this hand vision. Yeah. Safety first. R. Woohoo. No, man. Safety third. Hey, heat. Hey. Hey, what a what the conditions today. I mean, playing with new toys is always awesome, but it always takes some time to adapt to it and to get get the hang of it. And especially behind the pier, it was super gusty. There was a lot of people out there. >> It was changing a lot. Yeah. >> How was your session? >> The beginning was really nice. I think the first half an hour, I really enjoyed. And then the wind shifted a bit. It got a bit cloudy and changed a bit. >> But you got some bangers in. >> I think so. I tried. Yeah. >> What was your best feeling trick? >> I think I did a few jumps over the Power Winger. I don't know what to say. That one looked >> That's easy. Yeah, but the shop looks really cool. >> Nice. >> So, that was nice. >> Yeah. I mean, I really like the the feel of the of the End Vision uh the Q series. You can just feel it's cheaper, less performance. No, I'm just kidding. I mean, you can definitely feel a difference. And I think that's that's the whole point of uh making a high performance product and a mainstream product. But also, the fuel on the bar was absolutely great. The throw was a little bit too far for me. So, that's why I was struggling a few times. But, uh I think they're making it shorter and adding a stopper ball. So, uh I think that solves all the problem. >> Yeah. Yeah. And especially when the wind got a little gust here, then then you can really see the different like you really feel the difference and you can see the difference in the Alula that it just holds the the gust a little easier. And like I was on that time we just switched I think at that point when the when the wind uh got a big gustier and then I was like you were having a lot more fun and in an easier way than I was doing. >> Yeah. It seems to adapt much easier and recover much quicker and >> Yeah, for sure. Yeah. Yeah, you were still looping and I was just doing jumps and like >> and I also made the mistake of riding the the 50 cm bar instead of the 55 cm bar on the Q Series 9. I should have listened to you. >> Rookie moves. >> Rookie moves. Call me anytime. >> But yeah, overall I think it's a great lineup. I cannot wait to uh to get a few myself. Some Envisions. I think I will get the eight, the nine, and maybe a 10. How's the 12 on that thing? >> Um I personally only ride the smaller sizes. So like a SE I have a seven, eight, and a nine. >> I really like these three sizes. Um the 12 I usually don't kite or I go foil. >> Okay. Yeah. Um, if you have any questions, feel free to ask us anything in the comments and we'll get right back to you. I hope you enjoy your sessions coming up and much love. Thanks for watching again. Ciao. structured_review.summary: The 2026 Naish Pivot Nvision brings a 10% weight reduction over its predecessor thanks to Aluula and Quadtex construction, delivering faster kite loops, improved drift in waves, and better gust handling compared to the standard Q-Series Pivot. The lighter leading edge shifts the center of weight rearward when unloaded, creating a rare combination of forward drive under load and genuine drift for wave riding. It rewards riders who push hard, but demands more precision than the more forgiving Q-Series.
pros: 10% lighter than 2025 Nvision — noticeable performance gain
Superior gust absorption and quicker recovery versus standard Dacron version
Unique drift-when-unloaded behaviour rare in high-performance kites
Faster kite loops with quicker forward drive when loaded
SK99 bridle lines reduce drag and improve directness
New closing seam improves canopy twist for lighter bar feel and smoother turns
cons: Significantly more expensive than the Q-Series equivalent
Less forgiving than the Q-Series — demands precise, hard riding
Bar throw on tested setup was too long for some riders (stopper ball fix needed)
Smaller sizes dominate the sweet spot; larger sizes (12m+) may suit the Psycho better
structured_review.best_for: Intermediate-to-advanced freeriders and wave riders who want a premium all-around kite with genuine drift, quick loops, and extended wind range in a lightweight package.
structured_review.not_for: Budget-conscious riders or those who prefer a more forgiving, beginner-friendly kite that doesn't punish sloppy technique.
structured_review.rec_blurb: Lighter, faster, and surprisingly drifty — the Nvision is the Pivot for riders who want it all.
